title: A Sunday at the Street Market
summary: A narrator visits a lively Sunday street market in a small English town and describes the colourful stalls, wonderful smells, and friendly people she meets along the way.
body: Every Sunday morning, I walk down to the market in the centre of town. The streets are busier than usual, and the air smells of fresh bread and roasting coffee. Rows of wooden stalls line both sides of the road. Some stalls sell fruit and vegetables, while others sell handmade jewellery, old books, and second-hand clothes. I usually stop at the cheese stall first because the farmer always gives me a free sample. Today he had a sharp cheddar that was absolutely delicious. After that, I picked up a bunch of flowers from a young woman who was selling them out of a blue bicycle basket. She smiled and wrapped them in brown paper for me. Further along the street, a man was playing the violin while people stopped to listen and drop coins into his open case. The smell of hot doughnuts drifted from a nearby van, so I bought one and ate it as I walked. By midday, my bag was full and my feet were tired, but I felt happy. There is something special about a market — the noise, the colours, and the feeling that everyone is there to enjoy themselves.

annotations.exampleEn: The café is busier than usual on weekends.

annotations.phrase: line both sides of the road
annotations.meaningVi: xếp dọc cả hai bên đường
annotations.exampleEn: Trees line both sides of the avenue.

annotations.meaningVi: đồ cũ, đồ đã qua sử dụng
annotations.exampleEn: She bought a second-hand bicycle at a great price.
The bakery gave out free samples of their new cake.

annotations.meaningVi: phô mai cheddar có vị đậm đà
annotations.exampleEn: I prefer sharp cheddar to mild cheese on toast.

annotations.meaningVi: mua (một thứ gì đó) một cách thông thường
annotations.exampleEn: I picked up some milk on my way home.

annotations.meaningVi: lan toả từ, toả ra từ
annotations.exampleEn: Music drifted from the open window.

annotations.meaningVi: Vào buổi trưa, đến giữa trưa
annotations.exampleEn: By midday the sun was very strong.
People drop coins into the fountain to make a wish.
After the good news, she felt happy all day.

grammarNotes.title: Comparatives with -er than
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Để so sánh hai sự vật, ta thêm '-er' vào tính từ ngắn và dùng 'than'. Ví dụ: 'busier than usual' (đông hơn bình thường), 'older than me' (lớn tuổi hơn tôi).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Add '-er' to a short adjective and use 'than' to compare two things. Example: 'The streets are busier than usual.' For adjectives ending in -y, change y to i before adding -er: busy → busier.

grammarNotes.title: Past continuous for background action
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì quá khứ tiếp diễn (was/were + V-ing) diễn tả hành động đang xảy ra trong quá khứ, thường dùng làm bối cảnh cho câu chuyện. Ví dụ: 'A man was playing the violin while people stopped to listen.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use was/were + verb-ing to describe an ongoing background action in the past. Example: 'A man was playing the violin while people stopped to listen.' This sets the scene rather than stating a completed event.

translationVi: Mỗi sáng Chủ nhật, tôi đi bộ xuống chợ ở trung tâm thành phố. Đường phố đông đúc hơn bình thường, và không khí thơm mùi bánh mì tươi và cà phê rang. Những hàng quầy gỗ xếp dọc hai bên đường. Một số quầy bán hoa quả và rau củ, trong khi những quầy khác bán đồ trang sức thủ công, sách cũ và quần áo đã qua sử dụng. Tôi thường dừng ở quầy pho mát trước tiên vì người nông dân luôn cho tôi một mẫu thử miễn phí. Hôm nay ông ấy có một loại cheddar có vị đậm đà thực sự rất ngon. Sau đó, tôi mua một bó hoa từ một cô gái trẻ đang bán hoa từ giỏ xe đạp màu xanh. Cô ấy mỉm cười và gói hoa trong giấy nâu cho tôi. Đi xa hơn trên con phố, một người đàn ông đang chơi đàn violin trong khi mọi người dừng lại nghe và thả tiền xu vào hộp đàn mở. Mùi bánh donut nóng từ một chiếc xe tải gần đó lan toả ra, vì vậy tôi mua một cái và ăn trong khi đi. Đến trưa, túi tôi đã đầy và chân tôi mệt, nhưng tôi cảm thấy vui. Có gì đó đặc biệt về một khu chợ — tiếng ồn ào, màu sắc, và cảm giác rằng mọi người đều đến đó để tận hưởng.

questions.prompt: When does the narrator visit the market?
questions.explanation: The narrator states: 'Every Sunday morning, I walk down to the market.'
questions.hint: Listen for the day and the time of day.

questions.prompt: What does the air smell of at the market?
questions.explanation: The text says 'the air smells of fresh bread and roasting coffee.'
questions.hint: Two smells are mentioned at the very beginning.

questions.prompt: Why does the narrator always stop at the cheese stall first?
options: Because it is the cheapest stall
Because the farmer gives a free sample
Because she works there on weekends
Because it sells the best fruit
questions.explanation: She stops there 'because the farmer always gives me a free sample.'
questions.hint: What does the farmer give her for free?

questions.prompt: What kind of cheese did the narrator try today?
questions.explanation: The narrator says 'he had a sharp cheddar that was absolutely delicious.'
questions.hint: It is described as 'sharp' and a specific type.

questions.prompt: How was the young woman selling flowers?
questions.explanation: The flowers were being sold 'out of a blue bicycle basket.'
questions.hint: Think about what mode of transport is associated with the flowers.

questions.prompt: How did the flower seller wrap the flowers?
questions.hint: The material has a colour and is a type of paper.

questions.prompt: What was the man further along the street doing?
questions.explanation: A man was 'playing the violin while people stopped to listen.'
questions.hint: He was a musician — what instrument did he play?

questions.prompt: What did people do with coins near the musician?
options: They put them in their pockets
They dropped them into his open case
They used them to pay for food
They threw them into a fountain
questions.explanation: People 'drop coins into his open case.'
questions.hint: The musician had something open in front of him.

questions.prompt: What food did the narrator buy from the van?
questions.explanation: She smelled 'hot doughnuts drifted from a nearby van, so I bought one.'
questions.hint: It is a round, fried sweet snack.

questions.prompt: How did the narrator feel at the end of her market visit?
options: Happy, even though her feet were tired
Bored, because the market was too quiet
questions.explanation: She says 'my bag was full and my feet were tired, but I felt happy.'
questions.hint: Her feelings are described just before the final sentence.

questions.prompt: What does the narrator say is special about the market?
options: The low prices and large variety of goods
The noise, the colours, and the feeling that everyone is there to enjoy themselves
The fresh produce and the friendly vendors
The music and the smell of coffee
questions.explanation: She ends with: 'There is something special about a market — the noise, the colours, and the feeling that everyone is there to enjoy themselves.'
questions.hint: Look at the very last sentence.

cloze.template: Every Sunday morning, I walk down to the {{b1}} in the centre of town. The streets are {{b2}} than usual, and the air smells of fresh bread and roasting coffee. After that, I picked up a bunch of {{b7}} from a young woman who was selling them out of a blue bicycle basket. She smiled and {{b8}} them in brown paper for me. blanks.explanation: 'market' — the whole lesson is about the Sunday street market.
'busier' — the comparative form of 'busy'; streets are busier than usual on market day.
'line' — stalls line both sides of the road, meaning they are arranged in a row along it.
'second-hand' — meaning pre-owned or used items sold at lower prices.
'sample' — a small amount given free so you can try before buying.
'delicious' — meaning very tasty; the cheddar was absolutely delicious.
'flowers' — she picked up a bunch of flowers from the young woman.
'wrapped' — she wrapped the flowers in brown paper.
'violin' — the street musician was playing the violin.
'drifted' — smells drift gently through the air; it is a vivid, poetic word choice.
'happy' — despite tired feet, the narrator felt happy at the end of the market visit.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Street markets like this one are becoming less common as people prefer to shop online or in large supermarkets. Do you think street markets are worth keeping alive in modern cities? What is lost if they disappear?
